exploration_log.aha_moment: The optional overloads provide the exact pattern: JSON_HAS_CPP_17 guard, enable_if_t SFINAE constraint via is_variant, simple serialization. For from_json, the tuple's from_json_tuple_impl_base with index_sequence showed how to dispatch over compile-time alternatives at runtime index.
exploration_log.hindsight: Looking at the tuple from_json pattern upfront for the index_sequence dispatch approach would have saved time vs designing from scratch.

alternatives: Serialize as JSON array [index, value] (task requirement)
Use monostate marker for empty variants
key_decisions.decision: JSON array [index, value] as specified by the task

alternatives: Recursive template instantiation for runtime index dispatch
fold expression with if (I == idx) check over index_sequence_for
key_decisions.decision: fold expression approach: from_json_variant_impl with index_sequence<Is...> and fold (from_json_variant_alt<Is>(...), ...)
key_decisions.reasoning: C++17 fold expressions are simpler and cleaner than recursive template instantiation; consistent with the codebase's use of fold expressions elsewhere

solve_notes: Added `is_variant` type trait in `type_traits.hpp` (guarded by `JSON_HAS_CPP_17` + `<variant>` include). Added `to_json` using `std::visit` to serialize as `{index, value}` array and `from_json` using a fold expression over `index_sequence_for<Types...>` to dispatch to the correct `variant_alternative_t` at runtime. Both overloads are guarded with `JSON_HAS_CPP_17`.
task_text:
Add to_json and from_json overloads for std::variant

The conversion functions in `detail/conversions/to_json.hpp` and
`detail/conversions/from_json.hpp` cover standard library types
including `std::optional`, `std::pair`, `std::tuple`, and
`std::filesystem::path`, but do not include `std::variant`. Users
who hold typed alternatives via `std::variant` must manually match
the active alternative before serialization. Add `to_json` and
`from_json` overloads for `std::variant<Types...>` that serialize the
active alternative as a JSON array `[index, value]` (where `index`
is the zero-based `std::variant::index()`) and deserialize by reading
the index and constructing the corresponding alternative via
`std::get<N>`. Guard the overloads with `__cpp_lib_variant` or an
equivalent feature-detection check. Add a supporting `is_variant`
type trait in `detail/meta/type_traits.hpp` to gate the SFINAE
constraints.

thrash_preventing_defs.name: to_json (optional)
thrash_preventing_defs.reason: read: establishes the JSON_HAS_CPP_17 guard pattern, SFINAE style, and placement in the file — prevents an agent from inventing a different guard or placement

thrash_preventing_defs.name: from_json (optional)
thrash_preventing_defs.reason: read: establishes the from_json pattern for optional types — prevents agent from guessing the dispatch mechanism

tier_difference_reasoning: The minimum set covers all edited defs. The thrash-preventing set adds the existing optional overloads: an AI agent would proactively search for these to understand the exact guard style, SFINAE pattern, and placement convention before writing the variant versions. Without seeing them upfront, the agent would need to make a search call mid-implementation to verify the coding pattern.
